subject: english
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: A clear paragraph has one main idea, supporting sentences, and a neat finish.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: Why do some paragraphs feel easy while others feel muddled? Structure makes the difference.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Follow Topic sentence → Support → Closing. The diagram is a map for one focused idea.

sections.title: Concept: Paragraph
sections.body: A paragraph clusters sentences around one idea. The topic sentence announces it; support proves it.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: One paragraph, one job. Everything in it should help that job.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: A message to a friend about weekend plans works best when the main plan comes first, then details.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Topic: Libraries are quiet places to focus. Support with two reasons. Close by restating the benefit.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Topic sentence and write one for a paragraph about teamwork.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Clear paragraphs start with a main idea, add support, and finish cleanly so readers never get lost.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Which sentence in your last paragraph was the true topic sentence?

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Rewrite a messy three-sentence note into one clear paragraph using the diagram.

diagram_package.title: Clear Paragraphs
diagram_package.caption: Topic → Support → Close
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Clear Paragraphs connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Support and explain what those sentences must do.
